rows.notes: Centered page heading at top; text is clear and unambiguous.

Description fairly legible though abbreviated. Right-hand (far-right) pound column shows a clear '3', shillings column '10', pence looks like '8' but ink faint; column alignment checked against the subtotal line below.

Description is abbreviated and somewhat unclear; the small round mark appears in the pounds/shillings area and I have read this as zeroed entry. The numeric fields are not clearly present; chosen zeros because no distinct digits appear in the right-hand £ s d columns for this line.

Description read as an abbreviated college/annual entry. Rightmost pounds column shows a clear '4'; shillings read as '12' (valid range), pence as '4'. Slight ink variation on the shillings but alignment with column lines supports this reading.
Abbreviated collector name. Pounds column appears blank or zero; shillings '8' and pence '2' are legible and align with the rightmost columns.
Handwritten name is somewhat unclear ('Panie' vs 'Panie'/'Panie'); amount alignment is clearer: pounds blank/0, shillings '1', pence '8'. Slight smudge on the name reduces confidence.

Long descriptive entry; pounds column shows '1', shillings '18'. Pence area shows '11' with a small loop after it interpreted as halfpenny (0.5) — the fractional loop is faint but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.

Mid-page (left-of-right) £ column contains a clear '40' directly to the right of this description. Shillings and pence columns show '0' and '0' (blank ruled columns). Vertical ruling lines used to align columns; digits are crisp.

Name reading 'Sermon Baxter' / 'Sermon' is slightly unclear but likely 'Sermon Baxter'. Right-hand pounds column (far-right set) shows '1' with shillings and pence blank (zero). Column alignment checked against neighbouring rows.
Possibly 'E. Conventien Octr 5' — the text is abbreviated. Rightmost columns show '1 0 0'. The date/notation is faint but the amounts align clearly to the right columns.

Underlined subtotal on the far-right: pounds '58', shillings '17', pence '9' with a small halfpenny loop (interpreted as 0.5). Line is well aligned and serves as a clear total for the above right-side block.

rows.description: South Newg: vic d frut: annum: & Michl:
rows.notes: This appears as a left-side section header (long abbreviation). The handwriting is fairly legible but abbreviated; I treat as a header because it introduces the next group with its own left-side amounts below.

rows.description: South Newg: vic d frut: annum: & Michl:
rows.notes: A second instance of the same phrase appears with a clear left-side pounds '20' in the mid-column, shillings and pence blank. Numerals are crisp and align with the left £ s d columns.
Likely 'Bell of Beggars' or 'Bedel of Beggars' — description is legible enough. Left-side columns show '1' pound and '3' shillings clearly; pence blank.
Description 'Plate Gauge' read from script. Left-side columns show '5' pounds and '15' shillings clearly; pence blank. Good alignment with ruling lines.
Short centered header 'soluta' appears below the left-side totals; ink and placement are clear.
Centered subheading; handwriting legible though with abbreviations. No amounts in the center header column.

On the right-side block beneath the 'Do Rector...' header. Pounds '20' and shillings '6' are readable; pence looks like '8' though the final stroke is faint. Column alignment checked against other right-side entries.

rows.description: Argument Recd de South New: & Marton
rows.notes: Description lengthy and abbreviated. Right-hand pounds column reads '27' though the tens digit is slightly faded; shillings and pence are blank/zero. Alignment consistent with right-hand columns.

Handwriting of the description is unclear ('Stipend pno? Scherey'). Numerical reading of '33 6 8' is inferred from the rightmost three columns; digits are readable but there is some ink variation making the descriptive text uncertain.
Text 'David: Ditto' is legible; the right-hand pounds column shows '5' clearly, shillings/pence blank. 'Ditto' implies same kind of payment as previous row.

Single-word entry 'Stipend' with right-hand amounts '34 13 4'. The '4' pence digit is somewhat cramped but aligns with the pence column; shillings '13' is within valid range. Slight ambiguity in the tens digit of pounds but '34' fits the visible strokes.

Long abbreviated description; right columns read '17' pounds, '4' shillings. Pence area reads '5' with a small loop after it interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5). The fractional loop is faint, hence moderate confidence.
Another 'Stipend' line; right-hand columns show '19 13 4'. The digits are legible though pence digit is slightly smudged; column boundaries verified.

Abbreviated description indicating 'pro 1/2 part'. Right-side shows '6' pounds, '11' shillings, pence '2' with what appears to be a halfpenny loop (interpreted as 0.5). The fractional mark is faint; slight ambiguity whether pence is '2' or '2 1/2', but alignment supports 2 + 1/2.

Handwriting of the description is unclear ('pro pno their' is a best reading). The right-hand numeric trio '33 6 8' matches visible figures, though some digits are faded; alignment checked against ruled lines.
Repeated 'David: Ditto' entry visible again, with a clear right-hand '5 0 0'. Contrast is good for the numerals.
Right-hand line showing '26 15 0'. The pence column is blank. Description is a one-word entry 'Stipend' which is legible.

Final right-side entry before the page total. Pounds read as '13', shillings '8', pence '6' with a small loop after the 6 interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). The fractional mark is faint but consistent with other halfpennies on page.

Bottom-most grand total on the far-right: pounds '359' (three-digit number visible), shillings '3', pence '10' with a small halfpenny loop interpreted as 0.5. The total line is clearly underlined; column alignment verified across the page.
